tagline: Saved revisions with per-item Preview / Restore and an optional diff.
whenToUse: Version History is the revisions panel (or Right Drawer body) for any document: an FxList of saved versions, each showing a version label, the author's avatar, a relative time and a short change summary, with the newest carrying a "Current" Badge. Every past version offers Preview (load it read-only) and Restore, and an optional side-by-side diff slot renders beside the list for comparison. Restore always routes through a Confirmation Dialog — "Restore version {n}? Current state becomes a new version." — because restoring is undoable and additive, never a rewrite of history; it is the exact contract flexa-builder's own revisions UI follows. Reach for it wherever users need to review, compare and roll back to earlier saved states.
